description_long: This is a freestanding water cooler unit with a rectangular, metallic body and a large, dark blue water bottle mounted on top. The front features a dispensing area with two spouts, likely for hot and cold water, and a small drip tray beneath. Three additional water bottles, identical to the one on top, are placed nearby, suggesting storage or replacement options. The sides and back of the cooler have vented panels for cooling and access, with one side showing a small access door or panel. The water bottles are semi-transparent with blue-tinted plastic and a label featuring a landscape image. The overall appearance is utilitarian, suitable for public or office environments, with a focus on functionality and easy access to drinking water.
description: A metallic water cooler with a large blue water bottle on top and three spare bottles nearby, featuring dual spouts and a drip tray. The unit is vented on the sides and back, with a practical, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dispensing area with two spouts, drip tray, and three spare water bottles placed beside the cooler.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the vented panel and a single spare water bottle partially visible behind the unit.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting the large vented panel and all three spare water bottles grouped together.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a vented panel and the three spare water bottles clearly visible in the foreground.
attribute_dispenser_spout_type: dual lever spouts with push-down paddles
attribute_control_panel_design: Two colored push-lever spouts with a small drip tray below; no digital display or complex controls visible.
attribute_brand_logo: A landscape image label is visible on the water bottles, but no distinct brand name is readable.
